rationale.R1.1: The section opens by asserting its central contribution is demonstrating that role-conditioned inter-agent dialogue drives accuracy gains, then closes by concluding the principal driver is Reasoner-internal chain-of-thought rather than any property of agent decomposition. Reconstructing a coherent, defensible contribution claim is load-bearing for any review of this text.
rationale.R1.2: A direct logical contradiction spans the section: the framing claim ('role-conditioned inter-agent dialogue yields more accurate compositional answers') is falsified by the paper's own ablation, which recovers 4.9 of 5.6 gain points with Perceiver and Verifier replaced by passive templates. Cross-section coherence fails at the load-bearing level.
rationale.R1.3: The quantitative ablation evidence is structurally misaligned with the stated main claim. A 87.5% recovery of the accuracy gain under single-agent prompting is presented as supporting the multi-agent framing rather than refuting it; surfacing this misalignment is essential to the review.
rationale.R1.4: If the effective mechanism is CoT elicitation within one frozen VLM, the novelty delta versus prior chain-of-thought prompting work for VLMs is materially narrower than the multi-agent framing implies. The review must assess what genuine contribution remains.
